sections.textAr:
الطلاق على ثلاثة أوجه: أحسن الطلاق وطلاق السنة وطلاق البدعة

فأحسن الطلاق: أن يطلق الرجل امرأته تطليقه واحدة في طهر لم يجامعها فيه ويتركها حتى تنقضي عدتها وطلاق السنة: أن يطلق المدخول بها ثلاثا في ثلاثة أطهار

وطلاق البدعة: إن يطلقها ثلاثا بكلمة واحدة أو ثلاثا في طهر واحد فإذا فعل ذلك وقع الطلاق وبانت منه وكان عاصيا

والسنة ي الطلاق من وجهين: سنة في العدد فالسنة في العدد يستوي فيها المدخول بها وغير المدخول بها والسنة في الوقت تثبت في المدخول بها خاصة وهو: أن يطلقها في طهر لم يجامعها فيه وغير المدخول بها يطلقها في حال الطهر والحيض وإذا كانت المرأة لا تحيض من صغر أو كبر فأراد أن يطلقها للسنة طلقها واحدة فإذا مضى شهر طلقها أخرى

فإذا مضى شهر آخر طلقها آخرى ويجوز أن يطلقها ولا يفصل بين وطئها وطلاقها بزمان

وطلاق الحامل يجوز عقيب الجماع ويطلقها للسنة ثلاثا يفصل بين كل تطليقتين بشهر

masail.rulingAr:
الطلاق على ثلاثة أوجه: أحسن الطلاق وطلاق السنة وطلاق البدعة

فأحسن الطلاق: أن يطلق الرجل امرأته تطليقه واحدة في طهر لم يجامعها فيه ويتركها حتى تنقضي عدتها وطلاق السنة: أن يطلق المدخول بها ثلاثا في ثلاثة أطهار

وطلاق البدعة: إن يطلقها ثلاثا بكلمة واحدة أو ثلاثا في طهر واحد فإذا فعل ذلك وقع الطلاق وبانت منه وكان عاصيا

والسنة ي الطلاق من وجهين: سنة في العدد فالسنة في العدد يستوي فيها المدخول بها وغير المدخول بها والسنة في الوقت تثبت في المدخول بها خاصة وهو: أن يطلقها في طهر لم يجامعها فيه وغير المدخول بها يطلقها في حال الطهر والحيض وإذا كانت المرأة لا تحيض من صغر أو كبر فأراد أن يطلقها للسنة طلقها واحدة فإذا مضى شهر طلقها أخرى

فإذا مضى شهر آخر طلقها آخرى ويجوز أن يطلقها ولا يفصل بين وطئها وطلاقها بزمان

وطلاق الحامل يجوز عقيب الجماع ويطلقها للسنة ثلاثا يفصل بين كل تطليقتين بشهر
masail.rulingEn:
Divorce is of three types: the best form of divorce (ahsan), the Sunnah divorce, and the innovated divorce (bid'ah).

The best form of divorce is that the man divorces his wife with a single pronouncement during a period of purity in which he has not had intercourse with her, and then leaves her until her waiting period expires. The Sunnah divorce is that he divorces a woman with whom he has consummated the marriage three times in three periods of purity.

The innovated divorce is that he divorces her three times with a single statement, or three times in a single period of purity. If he does that, the divorce takes effect and she becomes irrevocably separated from him, and he is sinful.

The Sunnah in divorce has two aspects: the Sunnah regarding number and the Sunnah regarding timing. The Sunnah regarding number applies equally to a woman with whom consummation has occurred and one with whom it has not. The Sunnah regarding timing applies specifically to a woman with whom consummation has occurred -- that he divorces her during a period of purity in which he has not had intercourse with her. A woman with whom consummation has not occurred may be divorced during purity or menstruation. If the woman does not menstruate due to youth or old age and he wishes to divorce her according to the Sunnah, he divorces her once. When a month passes, he divorces her again.

When another month passes, he divorces her a third time. It is permissible to divorce her without separating the time of intercourse from the time of divorce.

Divorce of a pregnant woman is permissible immediately after intercourse. She is divorced according to the Sunnah three times, with a month between each pronouncement.

sections.textEn:
Divorce is of three types: the best form of divorce (ahsan), the Sunnah divorce, and the innovated divorce (bid'ah).

The best form of divorce is that the man divorces his wife with a single pronouncement during a period of purity in which he has not had intercourse with her, and then leaves her until her waiting period expires. The Sunnah divorce is that he divorces a woman with whom he has consummated the marriage three times in three periods of purity.

The innovated divorce is that he divorces her three times with a single statement, or three times in a single period of purity. If he does that, the divorce takes effect and she becomes irrevocably separated from him, and he is sinful.

The Sunnah in divorce has two aspects: the Sunnah regarding number and the Sunnah regarding timing. The Sunnah regarding number applies equally to a woman with whom consummation has occurred and one with whom it has not. The Sunnah regarding timing applies specifically to a woman with whom consummation has occurred -- that he divorces her during a period of purity in which he has not had intercourse with her. A woman with whom consummation has not occurred may be divorced during purity or menstruation. If the woman does not menstruate due to youth or old age and he wishes to divorce her according to the Sunnah, he divorces her once. When a month passes, he divorces her again.

When another month passes, he divorces her a third time. It is permissible to divorce her without separating the time of intercourse from the time of divorce.

Divorce of a pregnant woman is permissible immediately after intercourse. She is divorced according to the Sunnah three times, with a month between each pronouncement.

sections.textAr:
عند أبي حنيفة وأبي يوسف وقال محمد: لا يطلقها للسنة إلا واحدة

وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته في حال الحيض وقع الطلاق ويستحب له أن يراجعها وإن شاء أمسكها

فإذا طهرت وحاضت وطهرت فهو مخير: إن شاء طلقها وإن شاء أمسكها

ويقع طلاق كل زوج إذا كان عاقلا بالغا ولا يقع طلاق الصبي والمجنون والنائم وإذا تزود العبد ثم طلق وقع طلاقه ولا يقع طلاق مولاه على امرأته

والطلاق على ضربين: صريح وكناية فالصريح قوله: أنت طالق ومطلقه وطلقتك فهذا يقع به الطلاق الرجعي ولا يقع به إلا واحدة وإن نوى أكثر من ذلك ولا يفتقر إلى النية

وقوله: أنت الطلاق أو أنت طالق الطلاق أو أنت طالق طلاقا فإن لم تكن له نية فهي واحدة رجعية وإن نوى به ثلاثا كان ثلاثا

والضرب الثاني: والكنايات ولا يقع بها الطلاق إلا بينة أو دلاله حال وهي على ضربين منها ثلاثة ألفاظ بها الطلاق الرجعي ولا يقع بها إلا واحدة وهي قوله: اعتدي واستبرئي رحمك وأنت واحدة

masail.rulingAr:
عند أبي حنيفة وأبي يوسف وقال محمد: لا يطلقها للسنة إلا واحدة

وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته في حال الحيض وقع الطلاق ويستحب له أن يراجعها وإن شاء أمسكها

فإذا طهرت وحاضت وطهرت فهو مخير: إن شاء طلقها وإن شاء أمسكها

ويقع طلاق كل زوج إذا كان عاقلا بالغا ولا يقع طلاق الصبي والمجنون والنائم وإذا تزود العبد ثم طلق وقع طلاقه ولا يقع طلاق مولاه على امرأته

والطلاق على ضربين: صريح وكناية فالصريح قوله: أنت طالق ومطلقه وطلقتك فهذا يقع به الطلاق الرجعي ولا يقع به إلا واحدة وإن نوى أكثر من ذلك ولا يفتقر إلى النية

وقوله: أنت الطلاق أو أنت طالق الطلاق أو أنت طالق طلاقا فإن لم تكن له نية فهي واحدة رجعية وإن نوى به ثلاثا كان ثلاثا

والضرب الثاني: والكنايات ولا يقع بها الطلاق إلا بينة أو دلاله حال وهي على ضربين منها ثلاثة ألفاظ بها الطلاق الرجعي ولا يقع بها إلا واحدة وهي قوله: اعتدي واستبرئي رحمك وأنت واحدة
masail.rulingEn:
According to Abu Hanifah and Abu Yusuf. Muhammad said: He should not divorce her according to the Sunnah more than once.

If a man divorces his wife during menstruation, the divorce takes effect, and it is recommended that he take her back. If he wishes, he may keep her.

When she becomes pure, then menstruates, then becomes pure again, he has the choice: he may divorce her or keep her.

The divorce of every husband who is sane and adult takes effect. The divorce of a minor, an insane person, and a sleeping person does not take effect. If a slave marries and then divorces, his divorce takes effect. The divorce of his master does not take effect upon his wife.

Divorce is of two types: explicit and metaphorical. The explicit form is his saying: "You are divorced" (anti taliq), "you are a divorced woman" (mutallaqah), or "I have divorced you" (tallaqtuki). By these expressions, a revocable divorce of one pronouncement takes effect, and no more than one takes effect even if he intended more. No intention is required for them.

His saying: "You are the divorce" or "you are divorced with the divorce" or "you are divorced a divorce" -- if he has no intention, it is one revocable divorce. If he intends three, it is three.

The second type consists of metaphorical expressions (kinayat). No divorce takes effect by them except with intention or circumstantial evidence. They are of two kinds: three expressions by which revocable divorce takes effect, and only one pronouncement occurs. They are his saying: "Observe your waiting period" (i'taddi), "purify your womb" (istabri'i rahmaki), and "you are one" (anti wahidah).

sections.textEn:
According to Abu Hanifah and Abu Yusuf. Muhammad said: He should not divorce her according to the Sunnah more than once.

If a man divorces his wife during menstruation, the divorce takes effect, and it is recommended that he take her back. If he wishes, he may keep her.

When she becomes pure, then menstruates, then becomes pure again, he has the choice: he may divorce her or keep her.

The divorce of every husband who is sane and adult takes effect. The divorce of a minor, an insane person, and a sleeping person does not take effect. If a slave marries and then divorces, his divorce takes effect. The divorce of his master does not take effect upon his wife.

Divorce is of two types: explicit and metaphorical. The explicit form is his saying: "You are divorced" (anti taliq), "you are a divorced woman" (mutallaqah), or "I have divorced you" (tallaqtuki). By these expressions, a revocable divorce of one pronouncement takes effect, and no more than one takes effect even if he intended more. No intention is required for them.

His saying: "You are the divorce" or "you are divorced with the divorce" or "you are divorced a divorce" -- if he has no intention, it is one revocable divorce. If he intends three, it is three.

The second type consists of metaphorical expressions (kinayat). No divorce takes effect by them except with intention or circumstantial evidence. They are of two kinds: three expressions by which revocable divorce takes effect, and only one pronouncement occurs. They are his saying: "Observe your waiting period" (i'taddi), "purify your womb" (istabri'i rahmaki), and "you are one" (anti wahidah).

sections.textAr:
وبقيه الكنايات إذا نوى بها الطلاق كانت واحدة بائنة

وإن نوى ثلاثا كانت وإن نوى اثنتين كانت واحدة مثل قوله: أنت بائن وبتة وبتلة وحرام وحبلك على غاربك والحقي بأهلك وخلية وبرية ووهبتك لأهلك وسرحتك وفارقتك وأنت حرة وتقنعي وتخمري واستتري واعزبي واغربي وابتغي الأزواج فإن لم يكن له نية لم يقع بهذه الألفاظ طلاق إلا أن يكونا في مذاكرة الطلاق فيقع بها الطلاق في القضاء ولايقع فيما بينه وبين الله تعالى إلا أن ينويه وإن لم يكونا في مذاكرة الطلاق وكانا في غضب أو خصومة وقع الطلاق

بكل لفظ لا يقصد به السب والشتيمة ولم يقع بما يقصد به السب والشتيمه إلا أن ينويه

وإذا وصف الطلاق بضرب من الزيادة والشدة كان بائنا مثل أن يقول: أنت طالق بائن أو طالق أشد الطلاق أو أفحش الطلاق أو طلاق الشيطان والبدعة وكالجبل وملء البيت

وإذا أضاف الطلاق إلى جملها أو إلى ما يعبر به عن الجملة وقع الطلاق

masail.rulingAr:
وبقيه الكنايات إذا نوى بها الطلاق كانت واحدة بائنة

وإن نوى ثلاثا كانت وإن نوى اثنتين كانت واحدة مثل قوله: أنت بائن وبتة وبتلة وحرام وحبلك على غاربك والحقي بأهلك وخلية وبرية ووهبتك لأهلك وسرحتك وفارقتك وأنت حرة وتقنعي وتخمري واستتري واعزبي واغربي وابتغي الأزواج فإن لم يكن له نية لم يقع بهذه الألفاظ طلاق إلا أن يكونا في مذاكرة الطلاق فيقع بها الطلاق في القضاء ولايقع فيما بينه وبين الله تعالى إلا أن ينويه وإن لم يكونا في مذاكرة الطلاق وكانا في غضب أو خصومة وقع الطلاق

بكل لفظ لا يقصد به السب والشتيمة ولم يقع بما يقصد به السب والشتيمه إلا أن ينويه

وإذا وصف الطلاق بضرب من الزيادة والشدة كان بائنا مثل أن يقول: أنت طالق بائن أو طالق أشد الطلاق أو أفحش الطلاق أو طلاق الشيطان والبدعة وكالجبل وملء البيت

وإذا أضاف الطلاق إلى جملها أو إلى ما يعبر به عن الجملة وقع الطلاق
masail.rulingEn:
The remaining metaphorical expressions, if he intends divorce by them, constitute one irrevocable divorce.

If he intends three, it is three. If he intends two, it counts as one. Examples include his saying: "You are irrevocable" (ba'in), "decisive" (battah), "severed" (batlah), "forbidden" (haram), "your rope is on your shoulder" (habluki 'ala gharibiki), "return to your family" (ilhaqi bi-ahliki), "free" (khaliyyah), "innocent of me" (bariyyah), "I have given you to your family" (wahabtuki li-ahliki), "I have released you" (sarrahtuki), "I have parted from you" (faraqtuki), "you are free" (anti hurrah), "cover your head" (taqanna'i), "veil yourself" (takhammari), "conceal yourself" (istatiri), "be single" (i'zibi), "go away" (ighribi), and "seek husbands" (ibtaghi al-azwaj). If he has no intention, no divorce takes effect by these expressions unless they were in a discussion about divorce -- in which case divorce takes effect judicially, but not between him and Allah Most High unless he intended it. If they were not in a discussion about divorce but were in anger or a quarrel, divorce takes effect

by every expression that is not meant as insult or abuse. It does not take effect by what is meant as insult or abuse, unless he intended divorce.

If the divorce is described with some form of intensification or severity, it is irrevocable -- for example, if he says: "You are divorced irrevocably," or "divorced with the most severe divorce," or "the most obscene divorce," or "the devil's divorce," or "the innovated divorce," or "like a mountain," or "filling the house."

If he attributes divorce to her whole body or to a part that represents the whole, the divorce takes effect.

sections.textEn:
The remaining metaphorical expressions, if he intends divorce by them, constitute one irrevocable divorce.

If he intends three, it is three. If he intends two, it counts as one. Examples include his saying: "You are irrevocable" (ba'in), "decisive" (battah), "severed" (batlah), "forbidden" (haram), "your rope is on your shoulder" (habluki 'ala gharibiki), "return to your family" (ilhaqi bi-ahliki), "free" (khaliyyah), "innocent of me" (bariyyah), "I have given you to your family" (wahabtuki li-ahliki), "I have released you" (sarrahtuki), "I have parted from you" (faraqtuki), "you are free" (anti hurrah), "cover your head" (taqanna'i), "veil yourself" (takhammari), "conceal yourself" (istatiri), "be single" (i'zibi), "go away" (ighribi), and "seek husbands" (ibtaghi al-azwaj). If he has no intention, no divorce takes effect by these expressions unless they were in a discussion about divorce -- in which case divorce takes effect judicially, but not between him and Allah Most High unless he intended it. If they were not in a discussion about divorce but were in anger or a quarrel, divorce takes effect

by every expression that is not meant as insult or abuse. It does not take effect by what is meant as insult or abuse, unless he intended divorce.

If the divorce is described with some form of intensification or severity, it is irrevocable -- for example, if he says: "You are divorced irrevocably," or "divorced with the most severe divorce," or "the most obscene divorce," or "the devil's divorce," or "the innovated divorce," or "like a mountain," or "filling the house."

If he attributes divorce to her whole body or to a part that represents the whole, the divorce takes effect.

sections.textAr:
مثل أن يقول: أنت طالق أو رقبتلك طالق أو عنقك طالق أو روحك طالق أو بدنك أو جسدك أو فرجك أو وجهك

وكذلك أن طلق جزءا شائعا منها مثل أن يقول: نصفك أو ثلثك

وإن قال: يدك أو رجلك طالق لم يقع الطلاق

وإن طلقها نصف تطليقة أو ثلث تطليقة كانت طلقة واحدة

وطلاق المكره والسكران واقع ويقع طلاق الأخرس بالإشارة

وإذا أضاف الطلاق إلى النكاح وقع عقيب النكاح مثل أن يقول: إن تزوجتك فأنت طالق أو كل امرأة أتزوجها فهي طالق

وإن أضافه إلى شرط وقع عقب الشرط مثل أن يقول لامرأته: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق

ولا يصح إضافة الطالق إلا أن يكون الحالف مالكا أو يضيفه إلى ملك

وإن قال لأجنبية: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق ثم تزوجها فدخلت الدار لم تطلق

وألفاظ الشرط: إن وإذا وإذ ما وكل وكلما ومتى ومتى ما

ففي كل هذه الشروط إذا وجد الشرط انحلت اليمين إلا في كلما فإن الطلاق يتكرر بتكرار الشرط حتى يقع ثلاث

masail.rulingAr:
مثل أن يقول: أنت طالق أو رقبتلك طالق أو عنقك طالق أو روحك طالق أو بدنك أو جسدك أو فرجك أو وجهك

وكذلك أن طلق جزءا شائعا منها مثل أن يقول: نصفك أو ثلثك

وإن قال: يدك أو رجلك طالق لم يقع الطلاق

وإن طلقها نصف تطليقة أو ثلث تطليقة كانت طلقة واحدة

وطلاق المكره والسكران واقع ويقع طلاق الأخرس بالإشارة

وإذا أضاف الطلاق إلى النكاح وقع عقيب النكاح مثل أن يقول: إن تزوجتك فأنت طالق أو كل امرأة أتزوجها فهي طالق

وإن أضافه إلى شرط وقع عقب الشرط مثل أن يقول لامرأته: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق

ولا يصح إضافة الطالق إلا أن يكون الحالف مالكا أو يضيفه إلى ملك

وإن قال لأجنبية: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق ثم تزوجها فدخلت الدار لم تطلق

وألفاظ الشرط: إن وإذا وإذ ما وكل وكلما ومتى ومتى ما

ففي كل هذه الشروط إذا وجد الشرط انحلت اليمين إلا في كلما فإن الطلاق يتكرر بتكرار الشرط حتى يقع ثلاث
masail.rulingEn:
For example, if he says: "You are divorced," or "your neck is divorced," or "your soul is divorced," or "your body," "your person," "your private part," or "your face."

Likewise if he divorces an undivided portion of her, such as saying: "Half of you" or "a third of you."

If he says: "Your hand is divorced" or "your foot is divorced," the divorce does not take effect.

If he divorces her half a divorce or a third of a divorce, it counts as one full divorce.

The divorce of a coerced person and a drunk person takes effect. The divorce of a mute person takes effect by gesture.

If the divorce is attached to the marriage, it takes effect upon the marriage -- for example, if he says: "If I marry you, you are divorced," or "every woman I marry is divorced."

If it is attached to a condition, it takes effect upon the fulfillment of the condition -- for example, if he says to his wife: "If you enter the house, you are divorced."

The attachment of divorce is not valid unless the one making the oath is an owner of the marriage or attaches it to his future ownership.

If he says to a stranger: "If you enter the house, you are divorced," then marries her and she enters the house, she is not divorced.

The words of conditionality are: in (if), idha (when), idh ma (whenever), kull (every), kullama (each time), mata (whenever), and mata ma (whenever).

In all of these conditions, when the condition is fulfilled, the oath is dissolved -- except with kullama (each time), in which case the divorce repeats with the repetition of the condition until three divorces occur.

sections.textEn:

In all of these conditions, when the condition is fulfilled, the oath is dissolved -- except with kullama (each time), in which case the divorce repeats with the repetition of the condition until three divorces occur.

sections.textAr:
تطليقات فإن تزوجها بعد ذلك وتكرر الشرط لم يقع شيء وزوال الملك بعد اليمين لا يبطلها

فإن وجد في ملكه انحلت اليمين

ووقع الطلاق وإن وجد في غير الملك انحلت اليمين ولم يقع شيء وإذا اختلفا في وجود الشرط فالقول قول الزوج فيه إلا أن تقيم البينة

فإن كان الشرط لا يعلم إلا من وجهتها فالقول قولها في حق نفسها مثل أن يقول: إن حضت فأنت طالق فقالت: قد حضت طلقت وإذا قال: إذا حضت فأنت طالق وفلانة فقالت: قد حضت طلقت هي ولم تطلق فلانة وإذا قال لها: إذا حضت فأنت طالق فرأت الدم لم يقع الطلاق حتى يستمر ثلاثة أيام فإذا تمت ثلاثة أيام

حكمنا بوقوع الطلاق من حين حاضت وإذا قال لها: إذا حضت حيضة فأنت طالق لم تطلق حتى تطهر من حيضها

وطلاق الأمة تطليقتان حرل كان زوجها أو عبدا وطلاق الحرة ثلاث حرا كان زوجها أو عبدا

masail.rulingAr:
تطليقات فإن تزوجها بعد ذلك وتكرر الشرط لم يقع شيء وزوال الملك بعد اليمين لا يبطلها

فإن وجد في ملكه انحلت اليمين

ووقع الطلاق وإن وجد في غير الملك انحلت اليمين ولم يقع شيء وإذا اختلفا في وجود الشرط فالقول قول الزوج فيه إلا أن تقيم البينة

فإن كان الشرط لا يعلم إلا من وجهتها فالقول قولها في حق نفسها مثل أن يقول: إن حضت فأنت طالق فقالت: قد حضت طلقت وإذا قال: إذا حضت فأنت طالق وفلانة فقالت: قد حضت طلقت هي ولم تطلق فلانة وإذا قال لها: إذا حضت فأنت طالق فرأت الدم لم يقع الطلاق حتى يستمر ثلاثة أيام فإذا تمت ثلاثة أيام

حكمنا بوقوع الطلاق من حين حاضت وإذا قال لها: إذا حضت حيضة فأنت طالق لم تطلق حتى تطهر من حيضها

وطلاق الأمة تطليقتان حرل كان زوجها أو عبدا وطلاق الحرة ثلاث حرا كان زوجها أو عبدا
masail.rulingEn:
If he marries her again after that and the condition is repeated, nothing takes effect. The cessation of ownership after the oath does not void it.

If the condition is fulfilled during his ownership, the oath is dissolved and the divorce takes effect. If the condition is fulfilled outside his ownership, the oath is dissolved and nothing takes effect. If they disagree about the fulfillment of the condition, the husband's statement is accepted unless she produces evidence.

If the condition can only be known from her side, her statement is accepted regarding herself -- for example, if he says: "If you menstruate, you are divorced," and she says: "I have menstruated," she is divorced. If he says: "If you menstruate, you and so-and-so are divorced," and she says: "I have menstruated," she is divorced but so-and-so is not. If he says to her: "If you menstruate, you are divorced," and she sees blood, the divorce does not take effect until the bleeding continues for three days. Once three days are complete, we rule that the divorce took effect from the time she first menstruated. If he says: "If you complete a menstrual period, you are divorced," she is not divorced until she becomes pure from her menstruation.

The divorce of a slave-woman is two pronouncements, whether her husband is free or a slave. The divorce of a free woman is three, whether her husband is free or a slave.

sections.textEn:
If he marries her again after that and the condition is repeated, nothing takes effect. The cessation of ownership after the oath does not void it.

If the condition is fulfilled during his ownership, the oath is dissolved and the divorce takes effect. If the condition is fulfilled outside his ownership, the oath is dissolved and nothing takes effect. If they disagree about the fulfillment of the condition, the husband's statement is accepted unless she produces evidence.

If the condition can only be known from her side, her statement is accepted regarding herself -- for example, if he says: "If you menstruate, you are divorced," and she says: "I have menstruated," she is divorced. If he says: "If you menstruate, you and so-and-so are divorced," and she says: "I have menstruated," she is divorced but so-and-so is not. If he says to her: "If you menstruate, you are divorced," and she sees blood, the divorce does not take effect until the bleeding continues for three days. Once three days are complete, we rule that the divorce took effect from the time she first menstruated. If he says: "If you complete a menstrual period, you are divorced," she is not divorced until she becomes pure from her menstruation.

The divorce of a slave-woman is two pronouncements, whether her husband is free or a slave. The divorce of a free woman is three, whether her husband is free or a slave.

sections.textAr:
وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته قبل الدخول بها ثلاثا وقعن عليها فإن فرق الطلاق بانت بالأولى ولم تقع الثانية وإذا قال لها: أنت طالق وواحدة وقعت عليها واحدة

وإن قال لها أنت طالق واحدة وقعت واحدة وإن قال لها واحدة قبلها واحدة وقعت اثنتان

وإن قال واحدة بعدها وقعت واحدة وإن قال واحدة بعد واحدة أو مع واحدة أو معها واحدة وقعت اثنتان

وإذا قال لها: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق واحدة فدخلت الدار وقعت عليها واحدة عند أبي حنيفة وإذا قال لها أنت طالق بمكة فهي طالق في كل البلاد

وكذلك إذا قال أنت طالق في الدار وإن قال لها أنت طالق إذا دخلت مكة لم تطلق حتى تدخل مكة وإن قال لها: أنت طالق غدا وقع الطلاق عليها بطلوع الفجر

وإذا قال لامرأته:: اختاري نفسك ينوي بذلك الطلاق أو قال لها: طلقي نفسك فلها أن تطلق نفسها ما دامت في مجلسها ذلك فإن قامت منه أو أخذت في عمل آخر خرج الأمر من يدها وإن اختارت نفسها في قوله: اختاري كانت

masail.rulingAr:
وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته قبل الدخول بها ثلاثا وقعن عليها فإن فرق الطلاق بانت بالأولى ولم تقع الثانية وإذا قال لها: أنت طالق وواحدة وقعت عليها واحدة

وإن قال لها أنت طالق واحدة وقعت واحدة وإن قال لها واحدة قبلها واحدة وقعت اثنتان

وإن قال واحدة بعدها وقعت واحدة وإن قال واحدة بعد واحدة أو مع واحدة أو معها واحدة وقعت اثنتان

وإذا قال لها: إن دخلت الدار فأنت طالق واحدة فدخلت الدار وقعت عليها واحدة عند أبي حنيفة وإذا قال لها أنت طالق بمكة فهي طالق في كل البلاد

وكذلك إذا قال أنت طالق في الدار وإن قال لها أنت طالق إذا دخلت مكة لم تطلق حتى تدخل مكة وإن قال لها: أنت طالق غدا وقع الطلاق عليها بطلوع الفجر

وإذا قال لامرأته:: اختاري نفسك ينوي بذلك الطلاق أو قال لها: طلقي نفسك فلها أن تطلق نفسها ما دامت في مجلسها ذلك فإن قامت منه أو أخذت في عمل آخر خرج الأمر من يدها وإن اختارت نفسها في قوله: اختاري كانت
masail.rulingEn:
If a man divorces his wife before consummation with three pronouncements, they all take effect upon her. If he separates the pronouncements, she becomes irrevocably separated by the first and the second does not take effect. If he says to her: "You are divorced, and one," one takes effect upon her.

If he says: "You are divorced, one," one takes effect. If he says: "One before which is one," two take effect.

If he says: "One after which is one," one takes effect. If he says: "One after one" or "with one" or "along with one," two take effect.

If he says to her: "If you enter the house, you are divorced and one," and she enters the house, one takes effect upon her according to Abu Hanifah. If he says to her: "You are divorced in Makkah," she is divorced in all lands.

Likewise if he says: "You are divorced in the house." If he says to her: "You are divorced when you enter Makkah," she is not divorced until she enters Makkah. If he says to her: "You are divorced tomorrow," the divorce takes effect upon her at dawn.

If a man says to his wife: "Choose yourself" -- intending divorce by it -- or says to her: "Divorce yourself," she may divorce herself as long as she remains in that sitting. If she stands up from it or begins another activity, the matter passes from her hand. If she chooses herself with his words "choose yourself," it counts as

sections.textEn:
If a man divorces his wife before consummation with three pronouncements, they all take effect upon her. If he separates the pronouncements, she becomes irrevocably separated by the first and the second does not take effect. If he says to her: "You are divorced, and one," one takes effect upon her.

If he says: "You are divorced, one," one takes effect. If he says: "One before which is one," two take effect.

If he says: "One after which is one," one takes effect. If he says: "One after one" or "with one" or "along with one," two take effect.

If he says to her: "If you enter the house, you are divorced and one," and she enters the house, one takes effect upon her according to Abu Hanifah. If he says to her: "You are divorced in Makkah," she is divorced in all lands.

Likewise if he says: "You are divorced in the house." If he says to her: "You are divorced when you enter Makkah," she is not divorced until she enters Makkah. If he says to her: "You are divorced tomorrow," the divorce takes effect upon her at dawn.

If a man says to his wife: "Choose yourself" -- intending divorce by it -- or says to her: "Divorce yourself," she may divorce herself as long as she remains in that sitting. If she stands up from it or begins another activity, the matter passes from her hand. If she chooses herself with his words "choose yourself," it counts as

sections.textAr:
واحدة بائنة ولا يكون ثلاثا وإن نوى الزوج ذلك

ولا بد من ذكر النفس في كلامه أو في كلامها وإن طلقت نفسها في قوله: طلقي نفسك فهي واحدة رجعية وإن طلقت نفسها ثلاثا وقد أراد الزوج ذلك وقعن عليها وإن قال لها: طلقي نفسك متى شئت فلها أن تطلق نفسها في المجلس وبعده

وإذا قال لرجل: طلق امرأتي فله أن يطلقها في المجلس وبعده وإن قال: طلقها إن شئت فله أن يطلقها في المجلس خاصة وإن قال لها: إن كنا تحبيني أو تبغضيني فأنت طالق فقالت: أنا أحبك أو أبغضك وقع الطلاق وإن كان في قلبها خلاف ما أظهرت

وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته في مرض موته طلاقا بائنا وهي في العدة ورثت منه وإن مات بعد انقضاء عدتها فلا ميراث لها

وإذا قال الزوج لامرأته: أنت طالق إن شاء الله متصلا لم يقع الطلاق عليها

وإن قال: لها أنت طالق ثلاثا إلا واحدة طلقت اثنتين وإن قال: ثلاثا إلا اثنتين طلقت واحدة

masail.rulingAr:
واحدة بائنة ولا يكون ثلاثا وإن نوى الزوج ذلك

ولا بد من ذكر النفس في كلامه أو في كلامها وإن طلقت نفسها في قوله: طلقي نفسك فهي واحدة رجعية وإن طلقت نفسها ثلاثا وقد أراد الزوج ذلك وقعن عليها وإن قال لها: طلقي نفسك متى شئت فلها أن تطلق نفسها في المجلس وبعده

وإذا قال لرجل: طلق امرأتي فله أن يطلقها في المجلس وبعده وإن قال: طلقها إن شئت فله أن يطلقها في المجلس خاصة وإن قال لها: إن كنا تحبيني أو تبغضيني فأنت طالق فقالت: أنا أحبك أو أبغضك وقع الطلاق وإن كان في قلبها خلاف ما أظهرت

وإذا طلق الرجل امرأته في مرض موته طلاقا بائنا وهي في العدة ورثت منه وإن مات بعد انقضاء عدتها فلا ميراث لها

وإذا قال الزوج لامرأته: أنت طالق إن شاء الله متصلا لم يقع الطلاق عليها

وإن قال: لها أنت طالق ثلاثا إلا واحدة طلقت اثنتين وإن قال: ثلاثا إلا اثنتين طلقت واحدة
masail.rulingEn:
one irrevocable divorce, and it does not count as three even if the husband intended that.

It is necessary that the word "self" (nafs) be mentioned in his statement or hers. If she divorces herself with his words "divorce yourself," it is one revocable divorce. If she divorces herself three times and the husband intended that, they take effect upon her. If he says to her: "Divorce yourself whenever you wish," she may divorce herself in that sitting and after it.

If he says to a man: "Divorce my wife," that man may divorce her in that sitting and after it. If he says: "Divorce her if you wish," he may divorce her in that sitting only. If he says to her: "If you love me" or "if you hate me, you are divorced," and she says: "I love you" or "I hate you," the divorce takes effect even if what is in her heart differs from what she expressed.

If a man divorces his wife during his terminal illness with an irrevocable divorce while she is in the waiting period, she inherits from him. If he dies after her waiting period has concluded, she has no inheritance.

If the husband says to his wife: "You are divorced, God willing" -- connected immediately -- the divorce does not take effect upon her.

If he says to her: "You are divorced three times except one," she is divorced twice. If he says: "Three except two," she is divorced once.

sections.textEn:
one irrevocable divorce, and it does not count as three even if the husband intended that.

It is necessary that the word "self" (nafs) be mentioned in his statement or hers. If she divorces herself with his words "divorce yourself," it is one revocable divorce. If she divorces herself three times and the husband intended that, they take effect upon her. If he says to her: "Divorce yourself whenever you wish," she may divorce herself in that sitting and after it.

If he says to a man: "Divorce my wife," that man may divorce her in that sitting and after it. If he says: "Divorce her if you wish," he may divorce her in that sitting only. If he says to her: "If you love me" or "if you hate me, you are divorced," and she says: "I love you" or "I hate you," the divorce takes effect even if what is in her heart differs from what she expressed.

If a man divorces his wife during his terminal illness with an irrevocable divorce while she is in the waiting period, she inherits from him. If he dies after her waiting period has concluded, she has no inheritance.

If the husband says to his wife: "You are divorced, God willing" -- connected immediately -- the divorce does not take effect upon her.

If he says to her: "You are divorced three times except one," she is divorced twice. If he says: "Three except two," she is divorced once.

sections.textAr: وإذا ملك الزوج امرأته أو شقصا منها أو ملكت المرأة زوجها أو شقصا منه وقعت الفرقة بينهما

masail.rulingAr: وإذا ملك الزوج امرأته أو شقصا منها أو ملكت المرأة زوجها أو شقصا منه وقعت الفرقة بينهما
masail.rulingEn: If the husband acquires ownership of his wife or a share of her, or if the wife acquires ownership of her husband or a share of him, the separation takes effect between them.

sections.textEn: If the husband acquires ownership of his wife or a share of her, or if the wife acquires ownership of her husband or a share of him, the separation takes effect between them.

sections.textAr:
إذا طلق الرجل امرأته تطليقة رجعية أو تطليقتين فله أن يراجعها في عدتها رضيت بذلك أو لم ترض

والرجعة أن يقول: راجعتك أو راجعت امرأتي

أو يطأها أو يقبلها أو يلمسها شهوة أو ينظر إلى فرجها بشهوة

ويستحب أن يشهد على الرجعة شاهدين فإن لم يشهد صحت الرجعة

وإذا انقضت العدة فقال: قد كنت راجعتها في العدة فصدقته فهي رجعة وإن كذبته فالقول قولها ولا يمين عليها عند أبي حنيفة

وإذا قال الزوج: قد راجعتك فقالت مجيبة له: قد انقضت عدتي لم تصح الرجعة عند أبي حنيفة

وإذا قال زوج الأمة بعد انقضاء عدتها: قد كنت راجعتها في العدة فصدقه المولى وكذبته الأمة فالقول قولها

وإذا انقطع الدم من الحيضة الثالثة لعشرة أيام انقطعت الرجعة وإن لم تغتسل وإن انقطع لأقل من عشرة أيام لم تنقطع الرجعة حتى تغتسل أو يمضي عليها وقت صلاة أو تتيمم وتصلي عند أبي حنيفة وأبي يوسف

وقال محمد: إذا تيممت انقطعت الرجعة وإن لم تصل

masail.rulingAr:
إذا طلق الرجل امرأته تطليقة رجعية أو تطليقتين فله أن يراجعها في عدتها رضيت بذلك أو لم ترض

والرجعة أن يقول: راجعتك أو راجعت امرأتي

أو يطأها أو يقبلها أو يلمسها شهوة أو ينظر إلى فرجها بشهوة

ويستحب أن يشهد على الرجعة شاهدين فإن لم يشهد صحت الرجعة

وإذا انقضت العدة فقال: قد كنت راجعتها في العدة فصدقته فهي رجعة وإن كذبته فالقول قولها ولا يمين عليها عند أبي حنيفة

وإذا قال الزوج: قد راجعتك فقالت مجيبة له: قد انقضت عدتي لم تصح الرجعة عند أبي حنيفة

وإذا قال زوج الأمة بعد انقضاء عدتها: قد كنت راجعتها في العدة فصدقه المولى وكذبته الأمة فالقول قولها

وإذا انقطع الدم من الحيضة الثالثة لعشرة أيام انقطعت الرجعة وإن لم تغتسل وإن انقطع لأقل من عشرة أيام لم تنقطع الرجعة حتى تغتسل أو يمضي عليها وقت صلاة أو تتيمم وتصلي عند أبي حنيفة وأبي يوسف

وقال محمد: إذا تيممت انقطعت الرجعة وإن لم تصل
masail.rulingEn:
If a man divorces his wife with one or two revocable divorces, he may take her back during her waiting period, whether she consents to that or not.

The taking back (raj'ah) is that he says: "I have taken you back" or "I have taken back my wife."

Or he has intercourse with her, or kisses her, or touches her with desire, or looks at her private parts with desire.

It is recommended that he have two witnesses for the taking back. If he does not have witnesses, the taking back is still valid.

If the waiting period expires and he says: "I had taken her back during the waiting period," and she affirms him, it is a valid taking back. If she denies him, her statement is accepted and no oath is required of her according to Abu Hanifah.

If the husband says: "I have taken you back" and she immediately responds: "My waiting period has expired," the taking back is not valid according to Abu Hanifah.

If the husband of a slave-woman says after the expiry of her waiting period: "I had taken her back during the waiting period," and the master affirms him but the slave-woman denies him, her statement is accepted.

If the bleeding of the third menstruation stops at ten days, the right of taking back is terminated even if she has not bathed. If it stops at less than ten days, the right of taking back is not terminated until she bathes, or until a prayer time passes over her, or until she performs dry ablution (tayammum) and prays, according to Abu Hanifah and Abu Yusuf.

Muhammad said: If she performs dry ablution, the right of taking back is terminated even if she has not prayed.

sections.textEn:
If a man divorces his wife with one or two revocable divorces, he may take her back during her waiting period, whether she consents to that or not.

The taking back (raj'ah) is that he says: "I have taken you back" or "I have taken back my wife."

Or he has intercourse with her, or kisses her, or touches her with desire, or looks at her private parts with desire.

It is recommended that he have two witnesses for the taking back. If he does not have witnesses, the taking back is still valid.

If the waiting period expires and he says: "I had taken her back during the waiting period," and she affirms him, it is a valid taking back. If she denies him, her statement is accepted and no oath is required of her according to Abu Hanifah.

If the husband says: "I have taken you back" and she immediately responds: "My waiting period has expired," the taking back is not valid according to Abu Hanifah.

If the husband of a slave-woman says after the expiry of her waiting period: "I had taken her back during the waiting period," and the master affirms him but the slave-woman denies him, her statement is accepted.

If the bleeding of the third menstruation stops at ten days, the right of taking back is terminated even if she has not bathed. If it stops at less than ten days, the right of taking back is not terminated until she bathes, or until a prayer time passes over her, or until she performs dry ablution (tayammum) and prays, according to Abu Hanifah and Abu Yusuf.

Muhammad said: If she performs dry ablution, the right of taking back is terminated even if she has not prayed.

sections.textAr:
وإن اغتسلت ونسيت شيئا من بدنها لم يصبه الماء فإن كان عضوا فما فوقه لم تنقطع الرجعة وإن كان أقل من عضو انقطعت

والمطقة الرجعية تتشوف وتتزين ويستحب لزوجها أن لا يدخل عليها حتى يستأذنها أو يسمعها خفق نعليه

والطلاق الرجعي لا يحرم الوطء وإذا كان الطلاق بائنا دون الثلاث فله أن يتزوجها في عدتها وبعد انقضاء عدتها

وإن كان الطلاق ثلاثا في الحرة أو اثنتين في الأمة لم تحل له حتى تنكح زوجا غيره نكاحا صحيحا ويدخل بها ثم يطلقها أو يموت عنها

والصبي المراهق في التحليل كالبالغ

ووطء المولى أمته لا يحللها وإذا تزوجها بشرط التحليل فالنكاح مكروه فإن طلقها بعد وطئها حلت للأول

وإذا طلق الرجل الحرة تطليقه أو تطليقتين وانقضت عدتها وتزوجت بزوج آخر ثم عادت إلى الأول عادت بثلاث تطليقات ويهدم الزوج الثاني ما دون الثلاث من الطلاق كما يهدم الثلاث

وقال محمد: لا يهدم ما دون الثلاث

masail.rulingAr:
وإن اغتسلت ونسيت شيئا من بدنها لم يصبه الماء فإن كان عضوا فما فوقه لم تنقطع الرجعة وإن كان أقل من عضو انقطعت

والمطقة الرجعية تتشوف وتتزين ويستحب لزوجها أن لا يدخل عليها حتى يستأذنها أو يسمعها خفق نعليه

والطلاق الرجعي لا يحرم الوطء وإذا كان الطلاق بائنا دون الثلاث فله أن يتزوجها في عدتها وبعد انقضاء عدتها

وإن كان الطلاق ثلاثا في الحرة أو اثنتين في الأمة لم تحل له حتى تنكح زوجا غيره نكاحا صحيحا ويدخل بها ثم يطلقها أو يموت عنها

والصبي المراهق في التحليل كالبالغ

ووطء المولى أمته لا يحللها وإذا تزوجها بشرط التحليل فالنكاح مكروه فإن طلقها بعد وطئها حلت للأول

وإذا طلق الرجل الحرة تطليقه أو تطليقتين وانقضت عدتها وتزوجت بزوج آخر ثم عادت إلى الأول عادت بثلاث تطليقات ويهدم الزوج الثاني ما دون الثلاث من الطلاق كما يهدم الثلاث

وقال محمد: لا يهدم ما دون الثلاث
masail.rulingEn:
If she bathes and neglects to wash a part of her body: if it is an entire limb or more, the right of taking back is not terminated. If it is less than an entire limb, it is terminated.

The revocably divorced woman adorns herself and beautifies herself. It is recommended for her husband not to enter upon her until he seeks her permission or she hears the sound of his sandals.

A revocable divorce does not prohibit intercourse. If the divorce is irrevocable but less than three, he may marry her during her waiting period or after it has concluded.

If the divorce is three for a free woman, or two for a slave-woman, she is not lawful to him until she marries another husband in a valid marriage, and he consummates the marriage with her, and then he divorces her or dies.

A pubescent boy suffices for making her lawful again (tahlil), just as an adult does.

The intercourse of a master with his slave-woman does not make her lawful. If a man marries her on the condition of making her lawful, the marriage is disliked (makruh), but if he divorces her after consummating the marriage, she becomes lawful to the first husband.

If a man divorces a free woman once or twice, her waiting period expires, and she marries another husband and then returns to the first, she returns with three pronouncements of divorce. The second husband cancels what is less than three divorces just as he cancels three.

Muhammad said: He does not cancel what is less than three.

sections.textEn:
If she bathes and neglects to wash a part of her body: if it is an entire limb or more, the right of taking back is not terminated. If it is less than an entire limb, it is terminated.

The revocably divorced woman adorns herself and beautifies herself. It is recommended for her husband not to enter upon her until he seeks her permission or she hears the sound of his sandals.

A revocable divorce does not prohibit intercourse. If the divorce is irrevocable but less than three, he may marry her during her waiting period or after it has concluded.

If the divorce is three for a free woman, or two for a slave-woman, she is not lawful to him until she marries another husband in a valid marriage, and he consummates the marriage with her, and then he divorces her or dies.

A pubescent boy suffices for making her lawful again (tahlil), just as an adult does.

The intercourse of a master with his slave-woman does not make her lawful. If a man marries her on the condition of making her lawful, the marriage is disliked (makruh), but if he divorces her after consummating the marriage, she becomes lawful to the first husband.

If a man divorces a free woman once or twice, her waiting period expires, and she marries another husband and then returns to the first, she returns with three pronouncements of divorce. The second husband cancels what is less than three divorces just as he cancels three.

Muhammad said: He does not cancel what is less than three.

sections.textAr: وإذا طلقها ثلاثا فقالت: قد انقضت عدتي وتزوجت ودخل بي وطلقني وانقضت عدتي والمدة تحتمل ذلك جاز للزوج أن يصدقها إذا كان في غالب ظنه أنها صادقة

masail.rulingAr: وإذا طلقها ثلاثا فقالت: قد انقضت عدتي وتزوجت ودخل بي وطلقني وانقضت عدتي والمدة تحتمل ذلك جاز للزوج أن يصدقها إذا كان في غالب ظنه أنها صادقة
masail.rulingEn: If he divorces her three times and she says: "My waiting period has expired, I married, he consummated the marriage, he divorced me, and my waiting period has expired," and the time frame could accommodate all of that, it is permissible for the husband to believe her if he predominantly thinks she is truthful.

sections.textEn: If he divorces her three times and she says: "My waiting period has expired, I married, he consummated the marriage, he divorced me, and my waiting period has expired," and the time frame could accommodate all of that, it is permissible for the husband to believe her if he predominantly thinks she is truthful.
